methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Rangeview is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Cameron Schiller and Aeden Gasser-Brennan, having raised $5M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a sector universe of 12 peers in Robotics & Manufacturing, including 1 public company (277810.KQ) and 8 private firms such as 1X Technologies, Foundation Robotics, Divergent, Exotec, and Locus Robotics.

Stage-progression analysis is the primary applicable method, using the $5M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression. This yields an implied valuation of $12M. A peer-median approach could not be meaningfully applied; while 12 same-sector peers exist, insufficient clustering emerged within tight filters (same-stage, adjacent-stage, and similar valuation tier all fell below the three-datapoint threshold necessary for reliable median calculation). Relying on all-sector medians would introduce noise inappropriate for a frontier-tech early stage company.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $11M (low) to $14M (high), with a median of $12M. This range reflects the single applicable method and should be treated as indicative rather than conclusive. Key uncertainties include the thinness of directly comparable Seed-stage robotics benchmarks, execution risk inherent to the stage, and the sensitivity of the range to refinement of the 2.5x multiplier assumption. Further validation would benefit from additional comparable transaction data or downstream financing signals.
